aids.title: Encoder Size Formula
aids.content:
2^n : n Encoder

4:2 encoder = 4 inputs, 2 outputs
8:3 encoder = 8 inputs, 3 outputs

Outputs = log2(Inputs)

aids.title: Priority Encoder Chain
aids.content:
I7 -> checks first (highest priority)
  |
  v
I6 -> checks if I7=0
  |
  v
... (cascade down)
  |
  v
I0 -> checks if all above are 0

aids.title: Simple vs Priority
aids.content:
**Simple Encoder:**
- One-hot input only
- Undefined for multiple active

**Priority Encoder:**
- Multiple inputs OK
- Always outputs highest
- Has Valid bit

aids.title: Encoder Rhyme
aids.content:
Many inputs, few outputs tell,
Which one is high, the code will spell.
Priority picks the highest one,
Valid bit shows when there's none!
